title: Neon Pulse
loreNote: The explosive album opener. Fast-paced and driven by bright, arpeggiated synths, setting the stage for a vibrant night out in the city.

title: Static on the Wire
loreNote: A catchy, danceable lead single featuring detached vocals over a driving drum machine and shimmering analog synthesizers.

title: Concrete Angels
loreNote: A massive, triumphant stadium-ready rock anthem leaning heavily into Tyler's 'Human Metronome' timing and explosive 80s gated reverb snare.

title: The Overpass
loreNote: A driving, altitude-heavy anthem built entirely around Evan Wright’s prominent, melodic bass guitar.

title: The Horizon Line
loreNote: The sweeping, hopeful closer to Side A. Features a rare sibling harmony duet that builds into a massive crescendo of synth brass before abruptly ending at twilight.

title: Midnight Drivers
loreNote: The title track and the anchor of Side B. A moody, pulsing Darkwave track about finding sanctuary in a moving car at night.

title: Shatterproof
loreNote: A cold, rhythmic track heavily influenced by European industrial synth-pop, featuring a detached, robotic vocal delivery from Ryan.

title: Terminal Velvet
loreNote: Engineered utilizing The Cosmic Tidal Lock Sound™. A brooding, zero-gravity space-rock ballad showcasing Cassidy's crystal clear vocals and lush spatial reverb.

title: Digital Ghosts
loreNote: An ethereal, haunting duet with heavy spatial reverb. The rhythm section is reduced to a sparse, echoing click track, leaving plenty of empty space in the mix.

title: Fade to Dawn
loreNote: The epic 1987 album closer. Features a massive 50-second instrumental progressive build leading into a stadium-shaking guitar solo, transitioning the Darkwave tension into pure cathartic rock.

title: Midnight Drivers (Basement Demo)
loreNote: A raw, lo-fi 1986 cassette tape recording of Cassidy and Ryan writing the title track on an upright piano in their parents' basement.
